title: Check a 9 V battery's voltage
description: Use a digital multimeter to check a loose 9 V battery on a dry, non-conductive surface while wearing safety goggles.

dialogue.text: Place the 9 V battery, digital multimeter, and safety goggles on a dry, non-conductive surface. Put on the goggles. Set the meter to the 20 V DC range with the black probe in COM and the red probe in VΩmA.

Hold the battery by its sides. Touch the red probe to the positive terminal and the black probe to the negative terminal without letting the probes touch each other. A fresh 9 V battery should read around 9.6 V. Stop if it heats or leaks.
Power down the meter, remove the probes, and store the battery in a cool, dry place. Remove your goggles.
